extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r5, which requires (g6pd_deficiency AND immunosuppressed AND qt_prolongation). rec_drug_b can be set true only by r1 (priority 50, condition g6pd_deficiency), but r2 (priority 90, same conjunction as r5) forces rec_drug_b := false and wins on priority whenever (g6pd_deficiency AND immunosuppressed AND qt_prolongation) holds. Thus in the exact and only situation where rec_drug_a becomes true (all three true), r2 overrides r1 and rec_drug_b is false. Conversely, rec_drug_b is true only when g6pd_deficiency is true but the full conjunction is NOT satisfied (otherwise r2 wins), and in that case r5 does not fire so rec_drug_a is false (default). The conditions "all three true" (needed for rec_drug_a) and "g6pd true but not all three" (needed for rec_drug_b) are mutually exclusive, so (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) is unsatisfiable. diabetes and ckd_dialysis affect only rec_drug_c and are irrelevant. The property holds for the entire input space.
